steps.title: Convert the radical
steps.content:
Rewrite the fifth root with a rational exponent:
$$
\sqrt[5]{p^3}=p^{3/5}
$$
Then substitute $t=p^{3n-1}$.

steps.title: Equate the exponents
steps.content:
The equation becomes
$$
p^{3/5}=(p^{3n-1})^{8/7}=p^{8(3n-1)/7}
$$
Because $p>1$, the exponents are equal.

steps.title: Solve for the constant
steps.content: Solve the exponent equation: $$ \frac{3}{5}=\frac{8(3n-1)}{7} $$ $$ 21=40(3n-1) $$ $$ 21=120n-40 $$ $$ n=\frac{61}{120} $$ The answer is 61/120.
